name: Country-Style Greens
recipeIngredient: 2 bunches Fresh collard, turnip, or mustard greens
1 tablespoon Lard or rendered bacon fat
1/4 pound Smoked slab bacon or ham (optional)
Salt and freshly ground black pepper, to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the smoked slab bacon into 1/2-inch dice or use an equivalent amount of ordinary sliced bacon.
2. Trim and discard the tough stems from the greens. Place the leaves and remaining tender stems in a large bowl with lukewarm water, soak for 5 minutes, then rinse several times to remove grit and sand.
3. Melt lard in a large, heavy, nonreactive pot with a lid (avoid using an aluminum pot; use one with enamel coating if possible). Add onions and bacon, frying over medium-high heat until onions wilt and bacon starts to brown (about 5 minutes).
4. Add greens and water, bring to a boil, then reduce heat to medium and simmer until tender with slight crunch (approximately 20 minutes).
5. Uncover the pot, increase heat to high, and boil off excess liquid for about 5 minutes. Season with salt, pepper, and Dijon mustard to taste before serving.
Serves 6.
Note: This recipe is from "Regional American Classics" by California Culinary Academy, Chevron Chemical Company, 1987.
